info.title: Installing and configuring an iSCSI ISO target server

abstract.para: The iSCSI LIO Target is an open-source, kernel-level software framework in Linux that transforms a standard server into a high-performance storage controller by exporting local storage over a network. It acts as the server side of an iSCSI SAN (Storage Area Network), managing the communication between physical backstores and remote clients to provide seamless, block-level data access. An iSCSI SAN is a specialized high-speed network that provides block-level network access to storage.

topic.para: To install and configure the iSCSI LIO Target server, follow the steps in the procedure:

procedure.title: Installing and configuring an iSCSI LIO Target server

step.para: Install and enable the Linux SCSI target framework:
Prepare the storage.You should have an unpartitioned disk or a logical volume ready, for example at /dev/vdb.
